title: Engage Program Initiation Ceremony with Tata Technologies
content: Event Date: 12th June 2025 in C block New Auditorium at 2:00-3:30 pm Event brief description – The Department of Electrical, Electronics and Communication Engineering, Galgotias University organized “The Engage Program initiation ceremony with Tata Technologies” held at C block New Auditorium Galgotias University, marking the beginning of a collaborative effort between the university and Tata Technologies. The guests were Shri Pawan Bhageria ji, President of Global HR and Chief Human Resources Officer, Education & Strategic Projects at Tata Technologies, Mr. Manish Kohli, Vice President of EdTech and Alliances at Tata Technologies, Ms. Snehanjali S., Associate Manager for Academia Partnerships and Program Lead of the Engage Campus Program. It was graced by Dr. K.M. Babu, Honourable Vice Chancellor, GU, Dr. Manisha Chaudhary, Director CPDD, Dr. Sudhir Kumar Singh, Dean SOE, and Dr. Meenakshi Awasthi, Head DEECE. The event brought together 200 talented students from B.Tech Mechanical and Electrical & Electronics branches, who will undergo a unique 6 months training program. Event Detailed Description The Department of Electrical, Electronics and Communication Engineering, Galgotias University organized “The Engage Program initiation ceremony with Tata Technologies” held at C block New Auditorium Galgotias University, marking the beginning of a collaborative effort between the university and Tata Technologies. The guests were Shri Pawan Bhageria ji, President of Global HR and Chief Human Resources Officer, Education & Strategic Projects at Tata Technologies, Mr. Manish Kohli, Vice President of EdTech and Alliances at Tata Technologies, Ms. Snehanjali S., Associate Manager for Academia Partnerships and Program Lead of the Engage Campus Program. It was graced by Dr. K.M. Babu, Honourable Vice Chancellor, GU, Dr. Manisha Chaudhary, Director Placements, CPDD, Dr. Sudhir Kumar Singh, Dean SOE, and Dr. Meenakshi Awasthi, Head DEECE. The event brought together 200 talented students from B.Tech. Mechanical and Electrical & Electronics branches, who will undergo a unique 6 months training program. Key Highlights: - 100% Attendance Mandatory: Students are required to maintain 100% attendance throughout the program. The participants were undergraduate students from DEECE, Galgotias University. 240 entries were registered. Department Name – Department of Electrical, Electronics & Communication Engineering Event Outcome: The Engage Program initiation ceremony marked the beginning of a promising partnership between Galgotias University and Tata Technologies. By providing students with industry-relevant skills and knowledge, the program aims to empower them for successful careers and contribute to sustainable economic growth and development.
